verses.text: 'In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was God'; all things were made by Him. Eternal Son. Christ is God from everlasting, the Maker of all.
'In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was God'; all things were made by Him. Eternal Son. Christ is God from everlasting, the Maker of all.
'In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was God'; all things were made by Him. Eternal Son. Christ is God from everlasting, the Maker of all.
In Him was life, 'the light of men'; the light shines in darkness, which 'comprehended it not.' Light unquenched. The darkness cannot overcome the light of Christ.
In Him was life, 'the light of men'; the light shines in darkness, which 'comprehended it not.' Light unquenched. The darkness cannot overcome the light of Christ.
John came to bear witness of the Light, not being that Light; 'the true Light... lighteth every man.' A pointing finger. The herald exists only to direct men to Christ.
John came to bear witness of the Light, not being that Light; 'the true Light... lighteth every man.' A pointing finger. The herald exists only to direct men to Christ.
John came to bear witness of the Light, not being that Light; 'the true Light... lighteth every man.' A pointing finger. The herald exists only to direct men to Christ.
John came to bear witness of the Light, not being that Light; 'the true Light... lighteth every man.' A pointing finger. The herald exists only to direct men to Christ.
His own received Him not; but to as many as received Him 'gave he power to become the sons of God.' New birth. Faith makes us children of God.
His own received Him not; but to as many as received Him 'gave he power to become the sons of God.' New birth. Faith makes us children of God.
His own received Him not; but to as many as received Him 'gave he power to become the sons of God.' New birth. Faith makes us children of God.
His own received Him not; but to as many as received Him 'gave he power to become the sons of God.' New birth. Faith makes us children of God.
'The Word was made flesh, and dwelt among us... full of grace and truth'; we beheld His glory. The incarnation. God Himself came to live among men.
Of His fulness we receive 'grace for grace'; the law came by Moses, but 'grace and truth' by Jesus Christ. Surpassing gift. Christ brings what the law could not.

The priests ask who John is; 'I am the voice... Make straight the way of the Lord.' Lowly herald. He claims no greatness but his task.

Why baptize? he points to One among them 'whose shoe's latchet I am not worthy to unloose.' Deep humility. He counts himself unworthy to serve the Christ.

'Behold the Lamb of God, which taketh away the sin of the world.' The great theme. Christ is the sacrifice for the world's sin.
'Behold the Lamb of God, which taketh away the sin of the world.' The great theme. Christ is the sacrifice for the world's sin.
'Behold the Lamb of God, which taketh away the sin of the world.' The great theme. Christ is the sacrifice for the world's sin.
He saw the Spirit descend and abide on Him; 'this is the Son of God.' Heaven's witness. The Spirit marks out the Christ.
He saw the Spirit descend and abide on Him; 'this is the Son of God.' Heaven's witness. The Spirit marks out the Christ.
He saw the Spirit descend and abide on Him; 'this is the Son of God.' Heaven's witness. The Spirit marks out the Christ.
John points two disciples to the Lamb; they follow Jesus, who says, 'Come and see.' First followers. To seek Christ is to be welcomed by Him.

Andrew finds Peter, 'We have found the Messias'; Jesus names him Cephas. Brought to Christ. The found one brings another.

He calls Philip, who finds Nathanael; 'Can there any good thing come out of Nazareth? Come and see.' Doubt answered. Seeing Christ settles every doubt.

'Behold an Israelite indeed, in whom is no guile'; thou shalt see greater things, heaven open over the Son of man. Known fully. Christ sees the heart and promises more.
